channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about the siege outside South Korea's presidential compound
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A standoff between rival government forces outside the presidential compound in South Korea has been a startling development, even for observers used to the country's famously rough and tumble politics.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: South Korean anti-corruption agency asks police to take over efforts to detain impeached Yoon
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s anti-corruption agency has requested that police take over efforts to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after its investigators failed to bring him to custody following a standoff with the presidential security service last week.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Californians advised to be on high alert for ‘Amber Alert’ scam
item.description: The California Highway Patrol says a new scam is circulating where people pose as Amber Alert "representatives" and attempt to gather personal information.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intermittent fasting could slow down hair growth: Study
item.description: Intermittent fasting is a diet that cycles between fasting and non-fasting periods throughout the day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Inside the Golden Globes: What you didn’t see on television
item.description: BEVERLY HILLS, Calif. (AP) — The Golden Globes are a social affair with occasional breaks for awards. It’s a game of seconds inside the packed ballroom as A-listers attempt to get in as many hugs and hellos as possible before the cameras start broadcasting to the world again.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Winter blast of snow, ice and bitter cold grips the US from the Midwest to the East Coast
item.description: A major winter blast of snow, ice, high winds and plunging temperatures are stirring up dangerous conditions across the U.S. central and southern states all the way to the East Coast.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle And Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Latest: The Golden Globes kick off award season
item.description: Hollywood got dressed up as the Golden Globes returned for its annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television workers at the Beverly Hilton in Beverly Hills, California. The show serves as the ceremonial start to the 2025 awards season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The heaviest snowfall in a decade is possible as a wintry blast roils parts of the US
item.description: A blast of snow, ice, wind and plunging temperatures stirred up dangerous travel conditions in parts of the central U.S. on Sunday, as a disruptive winter storm brought the possibility of the heaviest snowfall in a decade to some areas.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, church leader asks for prayers for victims of truck attack
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — At a Mass celebrated Sunday at New Orleans' St. Louis Cathedral, just blocks from where a man sped down Bourbon Street in a deadly attack early on New Year's Day, a church leader acknowledged the sense of unease that remains and asked for prayers for victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why you shouldn't charge your phone to 100%, according to experts
item.description: It may be tempting to charge to 100%, but experts say 80% may be enough — and beneficial for your phone. Here's why.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: British man and a nursing assistant were the last victims identified in New Orleans attack
item.description: The coroner's office said all the victims died from blunt force injuries. The suspect in the attack, Shamsud-Din Jabbar, was fatally shot in a firefight with police.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Opening a savings account? Here's what to know
item.description: Annual percentage yields (APYs) on top high-yield savings accounts are currently 4.5% and above, according to Bankrate.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Millions to get higher Social Security payments from bill Biden is signing
item.description: The bill Biden plans to sign on Sunday will allow roughly 3 million people to receive higher Social Security payments.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Golden Globes: What to look for and how to watch on Sunday night
item.description: Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes return for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tech law in 2025: a look ahead at AI, privacy and social media regulation under the new Trump administration
item.description: Artificial intelligence harms, problematic social media content, data privacy violations – the issues are the same, but the policymakers and regulators who deal with them are about to change.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Lu, University of Michigan, KRON4

item.title: Data company figures out which airlines are most on time
item.description: Mexican airline Aeromexico had the world's best record for on-time arrivals in 2024, according to an annual ranking released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SpaceX to launch flight test of Starship, with all new upgrades out of Texas
item.description: SpaceX is launching its seventh flight test of Starship— a new generation ship with all new upgrades.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: In-N-Out opening new restaurants across several states in 2025 and beyond: Here's where
item.description: The burger chain, which does not franchise its restaurants, currently keeps its operations (mostly) to the Western half of the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: NYC subway burning suspect, Laken Riley’s killer among illegal immigrants accused of preying on Americans
item.description: Sebastian Zapeta, who allegedly set a woman on fire in the NYC subway, and Laken Riley's killer, Jose Ibarra, were among violent migrants arrested or convicted in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Carolina scheduling executions again after a pause for the holidays
item.description: South Carolina is beginning to schedule executions again after a pause for the holidays, with Marion Bowman Jr. set to be put to death on Jan. 31.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drawn to New Orleans' iconic street of celebration, a night of partying becomes a nightmare
item.description: The night, like countless others Bourbon Street has welcomed over the decades, started out ripe for celebration.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral begins by tracing 100 years from rural Georgia to the world stage
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter 's extended public farewell began Saturday in Georgia, with the 39th U.S. president’s flag-draped casket tracing his long arc from the Depression-era South and family farming business to the pinnacle of American political power and decades as a global humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JEFF AMY and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chicago Bulls plan to retire Derrick Rose's jersey next season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Derrick Rose wasn't sure how he will feel when sees his No. 1 hanging from the rafters. He was still trying to process the news. The Chicago Bulls announced Saturday they plan to retire Rose's jersey sometime next season.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW SELIGMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What we know about New Orleans terror attack survivors
item.description: Terrorist Shamsud-Din Jabbar’s New Orleans truck-ramming attack injured more than 30 people, their lives forever changed. Here's what we know about them.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Washington Post cartoonist quits after paper rejects sketch of Bezos bowing to Trump
item.description: A cartoonist has decided to quit her job at the Washington Post after an editor rejected her sketch of the newspaper's owner and other media executives bowing before President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: TODD RICHMOND, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What to know as snow, freezing rain and bitter cold heads through much of the US
item.description: A major winter storm began Saturday in the central U.S. and was forecast to move east over the next several days, producing heavy snow, significant ice and frigid temperatures, according to the National Weather Service.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chiefs finally depart KC for Denver amid ice storm after 4 hours spent waiting on the tarmac
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — The Chiefs finally departed from Kansas City International Airport for their game in Denver on Saturday after spending about four hours stranded on the tarmac amid an ice storm that blanketed the region ahead of an impending blizzard.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, ABC 4

item.title: Hundreds of animals killed in Dallas shopping center fire
item.description: A fire that broke out at a shopping center in Dallas on Friday morning killed more than 500 animals, most of which were small birds, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hillary Clinton, George Soros and Denzel Washington will receive the highest US civilian honor
item.description: Former Secretary of State Hillary Clinton, Democratic philanthropist George Soros and actor-director Denzel Washington will be awarded the nation's highest civilian honor on Saturday in a White House ceremony.
item.dc:creator: KRON4

item.title: Take these steps to protect yourself from winter weather dangers
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Winter weather brings various hazards that people have to contend with to keep warm and safe. These dangers can include carbon monoxide poisoning, hypothermia and frozen pipes that can burst and make homes unlivable.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: A U.S. District Judge denied a Jan. 6 rioter’s request to attend President-elect Trump's inauguration later this month, citing concerns over the Capitol insurrection which incited hours of chaos and violence in the nation’s capital.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Soldier who blew up Tesla at Trump hotel left note saying blast was to be a 'wake up call' for US
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A highly decorated Army soldier who fatally shot himself in a Tesla Cybertruck just before it blew up outside the Trump hotel in Las Vegas left notes saying the New Year's Day explosion was a stunt to serve as a “wake up call” for the country’s ills, investigators said Friday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, TARA COPP, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
